task: I need to document a near-miss at work and send a follow-up notice to the people involved. Can you help me put together a short draft that records what happened, what action is required, and any next steps in a straightforward way?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a short, straightforward draft you can use and adapt:

---

**Subject:** Near-Miss Notice and Follow-Up Actions

Team,

This note is to document a near-miss that occurred on **[date]** at **[location/area]** involving **[brief description of people/equipment/activity]**.

**What happened:**  
[Brief factual summary of the near-miss. Include only observable facts, not assumptions.]

**Immediate action taken:**  
[State what was done right away to make the area safe or stop the activity.]

**Required follow-up action:**  
[State what needs to be corrected, by whom, and by when.]

**Next steps:**  
- [Inspection / verification / retraining / equipment check]  
- [Any temporary control to remain in place until follow-up]  
- [Date/time for review or closeout]

Please confirm receipt and advise when the required action has been completed.

Regards,  
[Your name]  
[Title / Department]

---

If you want, I can also turn this into a more formal incident notice or a version that reads like a field safety report.

user_simulator.text:
Close, but too generic. I need something I can actually drop into a notice without it sounding like a template from nowhere.

Make it tighter and more factual. No fluff, no “please advise” language. Include:
- date/time
- exact location
- what was observed
- immediate control put in place
- required correction
- who owns the correction
- deadline or review time

And keep it short enough to send by email or text if needed. I don’t want a formal report essay.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter draft you can drop into a notice:

**Subject:** Near-Miss Follow-Up

On **[date/time]** at **[exact location]**, **[what was observed]** was identified as a near-miss.

Immediate control: **[describe control put in place]**.

Required correction: **[describe correction needed]**.  
Owner: **[name/role]**.  
Deadline / review time: **[date/time]**.

This item remains open until the correction is completed and verified.

If you want, I can also turn this into:
- a version for email,
- a text-message length version, or
- a field-style notice with a sign-off line.

user_simulator.text:
Still a little generic. Better, but it reads like a fill-in form with the edges sanded off.

I need it to sound like an actual notice, not a blank template. Tighten the language and make it more direct/factual. No filler, no soft closes.

Something like:

Near-miss recorded on [date/time] at [exact location]. [What was observed]. Area was placed under [immediate control]. Correction required: [required correction], assigned to [owner]. Due [deadline/review time].

That’s the level. Short. Clean. No extra commentary.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — here’s a cleaner version in that style:

**Near-miss recorded on [date/time] at [exact location]. [What was observed]. Area was placed under [immediate control]. Correction required: [required correction], assigned to [owner]. Due [deadline/review time].**

If you want, I can also give you:
- a slightly more formal version, or
- a text-message length version.

user_simulator.text: That works. Tight enough. I can use it.
